items.title: Is opposing Israel 'anti-Semitism'? France’s latest attempt to fight hate risks inflaming it
items.summary: The French government is backing a draft law based on the idea that "hatred" of Israel is inseparable from hatred of the Jewish people. Critics of the “Yadan law” warn the new legislation will not just muzzle legitimate criticism of Israel but could further fuel anti-Semitism in France.

items.title: Several injured by Israeli strikes on Lebanese city Tyre
items.summary: Israel has repeatedly struck the ancient southern Lebanese city of Tyre, where several inhabitants have been injured. But locals are reluctant to leave - citing fears than a temporary order would become permanent displacement. Israel has said that it would not be able to destroy Hezbollah without occupying the whole of Lebanon. FRANCE 24's Olivia Bizo reports from Beirut in Lebanon.

items.title: Live: Several blasts heard coming from northern Tehran
items.summary: Several blasts were reportedly heard in northern Tehran on Saturday as the Middle East war neared its sixth week. Meanwhile, Iranian and American forces are racing to recover the missing crew member from an American warplane shot down by Iran on Saturday. Follow our liveblog for updates.

items.title: Iran, US race to recover missing crew member of crashed American fighter jet
items.summary: Forces from Iran and the US are racing to recover a missing crew member from the F-15 warplane, shot down by Iran on Friday. US media reported United States special forces had rescued one of its two crew members.

items.title: Artemis II astronauts cross halfway point between Earth and Moon
items.summary: The Artemis II mission passed the halfway point between Earth and the Moon on Saturday and sped toward a planned lunar flyby, with NASA releasing initial images of Earth taken from inside the Orion spacecraft. If all proceeds smoothly, the four astronauts aboard the Orion could set a record by venturing farther from Earth than any human before.

items.title: Uganda in shock after 4 children killed in kindergarden
items.summary: First, Uganda is in shock after 4 children were killed in a brutal attack at a kindergarden in Kampala. Then, Sudanese military leader and head of state, Abdel Fattah al-Burhan, appoints Yasir al-Atta as the Armed forces new chief of staff. And, a week until the Beninese people head to the polls for the country's general election, the opposition parties are in crisis.

items.title: Two US fighter jets downed in Mideast, one crew member rescued
items.summary: A US fighter jet was shot down over Iranian airspace on Friday, triggering a search operation by Washington which rescued one crew member. A second US Air Force aircraft went down later in the day, according to a US official. The whereabouts of any other crew were not immediately known.

items.title: Trump seeks massive $1.5 trillion defence budget amid Iran war
items.summary: Engaged in a costly war against Iran, the White House unveiled its proposed budget for 2027 on Friday, asking Congress to increase the United States’ already massive defence spending by more than 40 percent over the course of a year. This would be a record in the country's recent history if Congress approves the proposal as is.

items.title: US fighter jet downed. Spencer Boyer speaks to France 24

items.title: Iran: US fighter jet downed, War “It’s not a show”, The Space Race, Will Syrians leave Germany?
items.summary: It’s been a week that began with President Trump addressing the nation for the first time since the Iran War began five weeks ago. Expectations that he might signal an end to the conflict—or announce a ground offensive or a split from NATO—were unmet. Instead, his 18-minute speech echoed his social media rhetoric: a mix of “we’re almost done” and threats of escalating attacks, including striking every Iranian power plant if a deal isn’t reached. On the Strait of Hormuz, he told allies simply to

items.title: Les astronautes d'Artémis 2 à mi-chemin entre la Terre et la Lune
items.summary: Les quatre astronautes de la mission Artémis 2 de la Nasa ont fait plus de la moitié du chemin entre la Terre et la Lune. Ils continuent, samedi, de se rapprocher de l'astre dont ils feront le tour en début de semaine prochaine, une première depuis 1972.

translation.title: The astronauts of Artemis 2 halfway between Earth and the Moon
translation.summary: The four astronauts of the Nasa Artemis 2 mission made more than half the way between Earth and the Moon. On Saturday, they continue to move closer to the star that they will tour early next week, a first since 1972.

items.title: Pourquoi la proposition de loi contre les "formes renouvelées de l’antisémitisme" fait polémique ?
items.summary: Une proposition de loi de la députée Caroline Yadan visant à lutter contre l’antisémitisme en France sera examinée à partir du 16 avril à l’Assemblée nationale. L'élue affirme que la "haine de l’État d’Israël est aujourd’hui consubstantielle à la haine des juifs". Les opposants au texte lui reprochent d'essentialiser les juifs et de confondre volontairement antisionisme et antisémitisme.

translation.title: Why is the bill against "renewed forms of anti-Semitism" controversial?
translation.summary: A bill by Congresswoman Caroline Yadan to combat anti-Semitism in France will be considered from 16 April in the National Assembly. The elected woman claims that the "hate of the State of Israel is today consubstantial to the hatred of the Jews". Opponents of the text blame him for making the Jews aware and deliberately confusing anti-Zionism with anti-Semitism.

items.title: Téhéran et Washington en quête du pilote d'un F-15E américain qui s'est écrasé en Iran
items.summary: L'Iran et les États-Unis sont engagés, samedi, dans une course pour retrouver un des deux occupants du premier avion américain à s'être écrasé sur le territoire iranien depuis le début de la guerre au Moyen-Orient. La Maison Blanche s'est bornée à dire que le président Donald Trump avait "été tenu informé" de la situation. Téhéran promet une "généreuse récompense" à qui livrerait le pilote.

translation.title: Tehran and Washington looking for an American F-15E pilot who crashed in Iran
translation.summary: Iran and the United States are on Saturday in a race to find one of the two occupants of the first American aircraft to crash on Iranian territory since the beginning of the Middle East war. The White House merely said that President Donald Trump had "been kept informed" of the situation. Tehran promises a "generous reward" to deliver the pilot.

items.title: En direct : plusieurs détonations entendues depuis le nord de Téhéran
items.summary: Un journaliste de l'AFP a entendu samedi matin plusieurs détonations depuis le nord de Téhéran, un secteur visé la veille par de puissantes frappes, alors que la guerre au Moyen-Orient est entrée dans son 36e jour. Par ailleurs, la banlieue sud de Beyrouth a été bombardée à l'aube au moins à deux reprises par l'armée israélienne. Suivez notre direct.

translation.title: Live: several detonations heard from northern Tehran
translation.summary: A reporter of the AFP heard a number of detonations on Saturday morning from the north of Tehran, an area targeted the previous day by powerful strikes, while the war in the Middle East entered its 36th day. Moreover, the southern suburbs of Beirut were bombarded at least twice at dawn by the Israeli army. Follow our direct.

items.title: Réouverture du détroit d'Ormuz : "On s'achemine vers un contrôle iranien sur le détroit "
items.summary: Selon l'économiste Rémi Bourgeot, on se dirige vers une réouverture du détroit d'Ormuz sous contrôle iranien avec un système de "péage" qui servirait les intérêts géopolitiques de l'Iran.

translation.title: Re-opening of the Strait of Ormuz: "We are heading for Iranian control over the Strait"
translation.summary: According to economist Rémi Bourgeot, we are moving towards a reopening of the Strait of Ormuz under Iranian control with a system of "peage" that would serve Iran's geopolitical interests.

items.title: La France "de plus en plus raciste" ?
items.summary: Victime d'une campagne de haine en ligne et visé par des propos racistes, le nouveau maire de Saint-Denis-Pierrefitte, Bally Bayayoko, dénonce une société de plus en plus raciste. Est-ce le cas? C'est la Question qui fâche que nous avons posée à Maboula Soumahoro, Maîtresse de conférences en civilisation anglophone à l’Université de Tours, spécialiste de la diaspora noire africaine, auteure d'un ouvrage intitulé Le Triangle et l'Hexagone, Réflexions sur une Identité Noire.

translation.title: France "more and more racist"?
translation.summary: Bally Bayayoko, the new mayor of Saint-Denis-Pierrefitte, is a victim of an online campaign of hatred and targeted by racist statements. Is that so? This is the question that we asked Mabula Soumahoro, lecturer in English-speaking civilization at the University of Tours, specialist in the African Black Diaspora, author of a book entitled The Triangle and the Hexagon, Reflections on a Black Identity.

items.title: Burkina Faso, Ibrahim Traoré fait le bilan de l'an 1 de la Révolution
items.summary: Au Burkina Faso, à l’occasion de l’AN 1 de la Révolution dite Progressiste Populaire (RPP), le chef de la junte du Burkina a accordé un entretien à des journalistes triés sur le volet …. Il a fait le bilan de son action depuis le coup d’état de septembre 2022 qui l’a porté au pouvoir et pour lui tout va bien.

translation.title: Burkina Faso, Ibrahim Traoré takes stock of Year 1 of the Revolution
translation.summary: In Burkina Faso, on the occasion of AN 1 of the so-called Progressive Popular Revolution (PRP), the head of the Burkina junta gave an interview to journalists sorted out on the .... He has taken stock of his action since the coup d'état of September 2022 which brought him to power and for him everything is fine.

items.title: Ce que l'on sait sur l'avion de chasse américain que l'Iran dit avoir abattu
items.summary: Ce vendredi, Téhéran a annoncé avoir abattu un "deuxième F-35 américain" en quelques heures. Si le premier a été démenti officiellement, Washington est resté silencieux sur le deuxième incident. En examinant les photos publiées par Tasnim, Info/Intox détermine que l'épave serait celle d'un F-15E Strike Eagle américain habituellement basé au Royaume-Uni. Quant aux images censées prouver que l'un des pilotes est désormais captif en Iran, il s'agit en réalité d'une vidéo décontextualisée.

translation.title: What we know about the American fighter plane Iran says it shot down
translation.summary: This Friday, Tehran announced that it had shot down a "second F-35 American" in a few hours. If the first was officially denied, Washington remained silent about the second incident. By examining the photos published by Tasnim, Info/Intox determines that the wreck would be that of an American F-15E Strike Eagle usually based in the United Kingdom. As for the images supposed to prove that one of the pilots is now captive in Iran, it is actually a decontextualized video.

items.title: RDC : Pascal Boroto, 24 ans, prix Voix d’Afriques pour un roman sur mémoire et guerre
items.summary: À seulement 24 ans, l’écrivain congolais Pascal Boroto remporte le Prix Voix d’Afriques 2026 avec son premier roman Le nom de ma mère. Un texte intime et engagé, inspiré par la figure de sa mère, journaliste disparue, et par son propre parcours dans l’est de la RDC. Entre mémoire familiale, guerre et quête d’identité, il incarne une nouvelle génération d’auteurs africains qui écrivent au croisement du personnel et du politique. Invité du Journal de l’Afrique sur France 24.

translation.title: DRC: Pascal Boroto, 24, Voice of Africa Award for a novel about memory and war
translation.summary: Just 24 years old, Congolese writer Pascal Boroto won the Prix Voix d'Afriques 2026 with his first novel The name of my mother. An intimate and committed text, inspired by the figure of his mother, a missing journalist, and by his own journey in eastern DRC. Between family memory, war and the quest for identity, he embodies a new generation of African writers who write at the crossroads of staff and politics. Guest of the Journal de l'Afrique sur France 24.

items.title: D. Trump menace l'Iran, Israël étend sa zone tampon au Liban et Cap vers la lune pour Artemis-2
items.summary: Alors que la riposte iranienne se poursuit et que la crise énergétique s’aggrave, Donald Trump a menacé l’Iran de frappes contre ses infrastructures civiles. En parallèle, Israël intensifie ses attaques contre le Hezbollah au Liban, tandis que la Knesset a adopté en début de semaine une loi controversée sur la peine de mort visant des Palestiniens. Dans ce contexte géopolitique tendu, la NASA a lancé la mission Artemis II, symbole d’une nouvelle course à la Lune face à la Chine.

translation.title: D. Trump threatens Iran, Israel extends its buffer zone to Lebanon and Cape to the moon for Artemis-2
translation.summary: As Iran's response continues and the energy crisis worsens, Donald Trump threatened Iran with strikes on its civilian infrastructure. At the same time, Israel is intensifying its attacks on Hezbollah in Lebanon, while the Knesset passed a controversial death penalty law against Palestinians earlier this week. In this tense geopolitical context, NASA launched the Artemis II mission, a symbol of a new moon race against China.

The Circular states that the Fourth National Agricultural Survey is aimed at rural households in the People ' s Republic of China, including rural agricultural producers and other households; urban agricultural producers; agricultural production units; villagers ' committees; and town and town people ' s governments. The main elements of the census include agricultural production conditions, food and food production, new quality of agricultural productivity, basics of rural development, living conditions of rural residents, etc. The standard time of the census is 2400 hours on 31 December 2026, and the data for the period is annual 2026. items.title: 全国人大常委会启动节约能源法执法检查
items.summary: 新华社北京6月5日电 为推动节约能源法全面有效实施，促进更高水平、更高质量做好节能工作，全国人大常委会5日启动节约能源法执法检查。 节约能源法自1998年1月1日实施，并于2007年、2016年、2018年进行了修改。 记者5日从全国人大常委会节约能源法执法检查组第一次全体会议上了解到，为全面深入了解法律实施情况，此次执法检查采取实地检查与委托检查相结合的方式，检查组将赴天津、江苏、湖南、广东、广西、四川等省（区、市）开展实地检查，并委托北京、河北、山西、吉林、上海、重庆等省（市）人大常委会对本行政区域内节约能源法实施情况进行检查。检查组将采取实地检查、听取汇报、组织座谈、典型调研、专题研究、抽查暗访等多种方式，全面准确了解法律实施情况，对带有普遍性的问题深入调查研究。 检查组将重点检查8方面内容：落实节能管理制度情况，推进重点领域节能工作情况，重点用能单位节能管理情况，推动节能技术进步情况，实施节能激励措施情况，与法律配套的行政法规、部门规章以及地方性法规的制定情况，法律实施中存在的其他问题，对节约能源法修改的意见和建议。

translation.title: The Standing Committee of the National People ' s Congress initiated an energy-saving law enforcement inspection
translation.summary: In order to promote the full and effective implementation of the Energy Conservation Act and to promote higher and higher-quality energy efficiency, the Standing Committee of the National People ' s Congress launched an energy conservation law enforcement inspection on 5 June. The Energy Conservation Act has been in force since 1 January 1998 and was amended in 2007, 2016 and 2018. On 5 May, journalists learned from the first plenary meeting of the Standing Committee of the National People ' s Congress ' s Standing Committee on Energy Conservation Law Enforcement Inspection Unit that, in order to obtain a comprehensive and in-depth understanding of the implementation of the law, the inspection took the form of a combination of on-site inspections and commissioned inspections, which will be carried out in the provinces (districts and municipalities) of Tianjin, Jiangsu, Hunan, Guangdong, Guangxi, Sichuan and Beijing, Hebei, Shanxi, Jilin, Shanghai and Chongqing, among others. The Unit will have a comprehensive and accurate understanding of the implementation of the law and will conduct in-depth research and studies on issues of a general nature, in a variety of ways, including on-site inspections, debriefings, organization of talks, typical research, thematic studies, spot-checking and unannounced visits. The Unit will focus on eight areas: implementation of energy-saving management systems; promotion of energy-saving efforts in priority areas; focus on energy-saving management in energy-use units; promotion of technological progress in energy-saving; implementation of energy-saving incentives; development of administrative, sectoral and local regulations in conjunction with the law; other issues in the implementation of the law; and suggestions and recommendations for changes to the energy-saving law.
